description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels mounted on a flat, platform-like base. The panels are arranged in a staggered, partially overlapping configuration, with some panels tilted at an angle and others lying flat. The base structure appears to be constructed from a combination of metallic and composite materials, with raised sections and block-like supports that provide stability and separation between the panels. The surface of the panels is dark and reflective, characteristic of photovoltaic cells, while the base is predominantly matte and textured. There are no visible electrical inverters or cabling, and the overall design suggests a modular, ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban environments. The installation is sizable, with a footprint large enough to accommodate several panels and structural supports.
description: A modular solar energy installation with multiple tilted and flat solar panels on a raised, textured base, featuring block-like supports and a dark, reflective panel surface.
description_view_0: From this view, the staggered arrangement of the solar panels and the raised, blocky supports on the base are clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the varying tilt angles of the solar panels and the linear, elongated structure of the base.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the back edges of the panels and the solid, textured base, with less emphasis on the panel surfaces.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, but from the opposite direction, showing the panel tilts and the distribution of supports.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are both flat and tilted at approximately 30 degrees, with tilt direction varying; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metallic (likely aluminum or steel) frame and block supports
attribute_array_configuration: Clustered arrangement of 4-6 panels in staggered, partially overlapping positions
